sanskrit:
लोके ऽस्मिन् द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ ।
ज्ञानयोगेन साङ्ख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम् ॥
transliteration:
loke 'smin dvi-vidhā niṣṭhā purā proktā mayānagha |
jñāna-yogena sāṅkhyānāṃ karma-yogena yoginām ||
literal_meaning: In this world there are two kinds of steadfastness, formerly declared by Me, O sinless one: through the yoga of knowledge for the Sāṅkhyas, through the yoga of action for the yogis.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that both the contemplative-intellectual path and the active-engaged path are valid, time-tested ways to realize the Self, removing the either/or anxiety that paralyzes decision-making.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance resolved—Arjuna shifts from frozen comparison to permission to act according to his innate temperament.
trigger_scenario: A college sophomore torn between majoring in philosophy (contemplative) or engineering (active) feels freed when a mentor says, 'Both can lead to fulfillment—pick the one that matches your nature.'
anchor_text: Think deeply or act fully—both roads lead to the same freedom.
